title: British singer Bonnie Tyler dies at 75, famous for 'Hero' and 'Total Eclipse of the Heart'
summary: The Welsh singer, known for her unmistakable raspy voice, died surrounded by her family.

title: United States and Iran intensify mutual military attacks and threaten the Strait of Hormuz
summary: Iranian forces attack US bases in the region and Washington responds by bombing infrastructure, raising tensions in the Persian Gulf.

title: Japanese Parliament advances reform of the Imperial House Law to allow adoption of male heirs
summary: The bill, which seeks to guarantee succession to the throne, will be put to a vote with support from the ruling party and divisions in the opposition.

title: Reiwa Shinsengumi party leader Taro Yamamoto resigns and announces his definitive retirement from politics
summary: Yamamoto leaves the leadership of the party he founded and retires from political life to recover his health, while the party plans to change its name.

title: NATO summit concludes with Trump's threats over Greenland and Iran and a controversial gift from Turkey to leaders
summary: The US president conditioned the deployment of troops in Europe, while it is reported that Turkey gave pistols with live ammunition to attendees.

title: Three foreign pitchers of the Yakult Swallows achieve a no-hit game in Japanese baseball
summary: The historic feat was achieved in a combined manner and is the first time it has occurred with a completely foreign rotation in the NPB.

title: Bankruptcy of payment processor Zendoshin affects 60 financial entities with multi-million dollar losses
summary: The company declared liabilities of more than 60 billion yen and revealed accounting falsification that lasted at least 20 years.

title: Vladimir Putin reinforces his determination to continue the war in Ukraine, according to recent analysis and statements
summary: A Financial Times report indicates that the Russian leader is running out of viable options, while Russian official sources confirm his willingness to keep fighting.

title: A fire at a shoe factory in Fujian province, China, leaves at least 28 dead
summary: President Xi Jinping ordered a thorough investigation of the incident to clarify the causes and prevent future tragedies.

title: Two high school students drown in the Yasu River in Shiga after trying to rescue each other
summary: The teenagers were swimming in the river when they were swept away by the current, and one died trying to save the other.

title: Scientists at the University of Tokyo discover the brain circuit responsible for social aversion in mice
summary: The study reveals the neural mechanisms that generate feelings of hatred toward other individuals, which could help understand social disorders.

title: Japanese government links subsidies to public universities with inflation and considers a tuition increase
summary: The Ministry of Education seeks to update the funding model to reflect current costs and inflationary pressure on institutions.

title: Typhoon No. 9 approaches the Sakishima Islands of Okinawa with potentially destructive winds
summary: The typhoon, of great intensity, is expected to impact the region on July 11, so authorities have issued evacuation warnings.

title: Empress Masako harvests silkworm cocoons at the Imperial Palace in Tokyo
summary: The empress participated in the traditional collection of silk cocoons and highlighted their bright green color during the activity.

title: Assembly of 'Hoko' floats begins for the Gion Festival in Kyoto
summary: The Naginata Hoko, which will lead the parade, was the first to start the assembly process for the festival's main procession.

title: A 'handyman' detained in connection with the murder of an IT executive for alleged welfare fraud
summary: The suspect, who advised on the crime, did not declare income and would have illegally received public benefits.

title: Defense presents new evidence in Ooshiki case to request a new trial for murder
summary: It is argued that it is impossible to strangle a person with a towel, questioning the defendant's confession.

title: Debate in Japan over the bill that penalizes destruction of the national flag
summary: Critics point out that the law violates the principle of criminal legality, while defenders ensure it fosters patriotism.

title: More than 60% of hydrothermal vent species are threatened with extinction, according to the Red List
summary: Deep-sea mining puts unique ecosystems at risk, including a marine snail endemic to a single region.

title: Japanese government reaffirms its respect for the Bank of Japan's independence in new economic guideline
summary: The inclusion of this clause aims to dispel any suspicion of political pressure on the BOJ's monetary policy.
